title: Gravity with Gravitas: A Solution to the Border Puzzle
Building the Resilient Supply Chain
methodology_summary: Conceptual framework development based on case studies and literature synthesis. Identifies four levels of supply chain risk and proposes resilience strategies.
key_limitations: Conceptual framework without empirical validation; limited to qualitative case evidence

title: Perspectives in Supply Chain Risk Management
methodology_summary: Comprehensive literature review classifying supply chain risks and mapping robust mitigation strategies across supply, demand, and process dimensions.
key_limitations: Review paper; does not provide new empirical evidence

title: Managing Risk to Avoid Supply-Chain Breakdown
methodology_summary: Practitioner-oriented framework categorizing nine supply chain risk types with mitigation approaches.
key_limitations: Practitioner-focused; lacks formal empirical methodology

title: Disruption Risk Mitigation in Supply Chains: The Risk Exposure Index Revisited
authors: Simchi-Levi, David; Schmidt, William; Wei, Yehua; Zhang, Peter Yun
methodology_summary: Mixed-integer programming and graph-theoretic analysis. Introduces Risk Exposure Index (REI) using TTR/TTS. Implemented at Ford Motor Company. CVaR optimization for worst-case analysis.
key_limitations: Ford implementation is single-firm case; TTR estimation requires expert judgment; linear model assumptions

title: On the Value of Mitigation and Contingency Strategies for Managing Supply Chain Disruption Risks
methodology_summary: Analytical modeling of single-product dual-supplier setting with Markov-modulated disruption process. Compares mitigation (pre-disruption) vs. contingency (post-disruption) strategies.
key_limitations: Simplified two-supplier model; assumes known disruption probabilities; single-product setting

title: OR/MS Models for Supply Chain Disruptions: A Review
authors: Snyder, Lawrence V.; Atan, Zümbül; Peng, Peng; Rong, Ying; Schmitt, Amanda J.; Sinsoysal, Burcu
methodology_summary: Systematic review of 180 scholarly works classifying OR/MS approaches to supply chain disruptions by decision type (evaluating, strategic, sourcing, contracts, inventory).
key_limitations: Review scope limited to OR/MS methods; does not cover ML, network science, or spatial approaches

title: The Severity of Supply Chain Disruptions: Design Characteristics and Mitigation Capabilities
authors: Craighead, Christopher W.; Blackhurst, Jennifer; Rungtusanatham, M. Johnny; Handfield, Robert B.
methodology_summary: Multi-method empirical study using case studies and simulation. Links disruption severity to supply chain design characteristics (density, complexity, node criticality) and mitigation capabilities (recovery, warning).
key_limitations: Small sample of case studies; simulation parameters based on expert judgment

title: An Empirical Analysis of the Effect of Supply Chain Disruptions on Long-Run Stock Price Performance and Equity Risk
authors: Hendricks, Kevin B.; Singhal, Vinod R.
methodology_summary: Event study methodology analyzing 827 supply chain disruption announcements. Measures cumulative abnormal returns and changes in equity risk using matched-pair controls.
key_limitations: Selection bias toward publicly announced disruptions; US-listed firms only; confounding events possible

title: Predicting the Impacts of Epidemic Outbreaks on Global Supply Chains: A Simulation-Based Analysis on the Coronavirus Outbreak
methodology_summary: Discrete-event simulation using anyLogistix platform. Models multi-echelon supply chain with epidemic-induced facility closures. Tests impact of closure duration, propagation speed, and reopening timing.
key_limitations: Simulation-based without empirical validation; simplified epidemic model; deterministic facility closure scenarios

title: The Network Origins of Aggregate Fluctuations
authors: Acemoglu, Daron; Carvalho, Vasco M.; Ozdaglar, Asuman; Tahbaz-Salehi, Alireza
methodology_summary: Mathematical modeling of production network with Cobb-Douglas technology and input-output linkages. Proves that microeconomic shocks do not average out when network is asymmetric. Uses US BEA input-output tables for empirical validation.
key_limitations: Assumes competitive equilibrium; linear approximation around steady state; static network

title: Catastrophic Cascade of Failures in Interdependent Networks
authors: Buldyrev, Sergey V.; Parshani, Roni; Paul, Gerald; Stanley, H. Eugene; Havlin, Shlomo
methodology_summary: Analytical framework and simulation of cascading failures in coupled networks. Uses percolation theory to derive critical thresholds for catastrophic collapse.
key_limitations: Assumes random interdependencies; two-network model; specific network topologies only

title: Local Indicators of Spatial Association — LISA
methodology_summary: Introduces LISA statistics decomposing global Moran's I into local contributions. Provides significance testing for spatial clusters and outliers.
key_limitations: Assumes spatial stationarity; sensitive to spatial weights matrix specification; multiple testing concerns

title: Ripple Effect in the Supply Chain: An Analysis and Recent Literature
authors: Dolgui, Alexandre; Ivanov, Dmitry; Sokolov, Boris
methodology_summary: Literature review distinguishing ripple effect from bullwhip effect. Categorizes approaches into optimization, simulation, control theory, and complexity methods.
key_limitations: Review paper; does not provide new empirical evidence; limited coverage of ML and spatial methods

title: Supply Chain Risk Management: A Literature Review
authors: Ho, William; Zheng, Tian; Yildiz, Hakan; Talluri, Srinivas
methodology_summary: Systematic review of 224 journal articles (2003-2013). Classifies risk types, risk factors, and risk management/mitigation strategies.
key_limitations: Coverage ends 2013; pre-dates COVID, AI/ML advances, and digital supply chain research

title: Supply Chain Risk Management and Artificial Intelligence: State of the Art and Future Research Directions
authors: Baryannis, George; Validi, Samir; Dani, Samir; Antoniou, Grigoris
methodology_summary: Systematic review mapping AI/ML techniques to SCRM lifecycle stages (identification, assessment, mitigation, monitoring). Identifies random forests and SVMs as most commonly applied.
key_limitations: Pre-dates GNN, transformer, and LLM applications to supply chain; limited empirical benchmarking

title: Supply Network Centrality, Firm Performance, and Complementary Assets
authors: Brintrup, Alexandra; Ledwoch, Agata; Barber, Joe
methodology_summary: Empirical network analysis of automotive supply chain. Computes degree, betweenness, and eigenvector centrality for ~1,000 firms. Tests association with firm performance and risk exposure.
key_limitations: Single industry (automotive); static network snapshot; performance measured via limited financial metrics

title: Analyzing the Resilience of Complex Supply Network Topologies Against Random and Targeted Disruptions
authors: Zhao, Kang; Kumar, Akhil; Harrison, Terry P.; Yen, John
methodology_summary: Simulation-based analysis comparing supply network topologies (random, scale-free, small-world) under random failure and targeted attack scenarios using percolation analysis.
key_limitations: Synthetic networks rather than real supply chain data; binary failure model (functional/failed)

title: A Supply Chain View of the Resilient Enterprise
methodology_summary: Conceptual framework based on multiple industry case studies. Identifies redundancy vs. flexibility as two resilience strategies and five supply chain elements for building flexibility.
key_limitations: Qualitative case-based; no formal empirical testing; US-centric examples

title: Structural Drivers of Upstream Supply Chain Complexity and the Frequency of Supply Chain Disruptions
methodology_summary: Survey-based empirical study of 467 manufacturing firms. Tests how horizontal complexity, vertical complexity, and spatial complexity drive disruption frequency using structural equation modeling.
key_limitations: Cross-sectional survey; self-reported disruption frequency; single-respondent bias

title: Towards Knowledge Graph Reasoning for Supply Chain Risk Management Using Graph Neural Networks
methodology_summary: Applies GNN-based link prediction and node classification on supply chain knowledge graphs for risk reasoning. Tests GCN, GAT, and GraphSAGE architectures.
key_limitations: Synthetic knowledge graph augmented with limited real data; scalability to full industry networks untested

title: ISO 28000:2022 — Security and Resilience: Security Management Systems — Requirements
methodology_summary: International standard specifying requirements for supply chain security management systems. Covers risk assessment, security controls, incident management, and continual improvement.
key_limitations: Prescriptive framework; does not provide quantitative methods or empirical validation

title: NIST SP 800-161 Rev. 1: Cybersecurity Supply Chain Risk Management Practices
authors: National Institute of Standards and Technology
methodology_summary: Federal guidance document providing multilevel C-SCRM framework spanning enterprise strategy, mission planning, and system implementation. Aligned with Executive Order 14028.
key_limitations: Focused on federal IT/cybersecurity context; prescriptive rather than analytical

title: Risk, Resilience, and Rebalancing in Global Value Chains
methodology_summary: Analysis of 23 industry value chains using proprietary datasets. Quantifies disruption frequency, cost, and geographic concentration. Survey of 60+ supply chain executives.
key_limitations: Proprietary methodology; limited reproducibility; McKinsey client bias in survey sample

title: Industrial Dynamics: A Major Breakthrough for Decision Makers
methodology_summary: Foundational paper establishing system dynamics methodology for supply chain analysis. Discovers demand amplification (bullwhip effect) through simulation of multi-echelon supply chain.
key_limitations: Simplified serial supply chain model; pre-dates modern global supply networks

title: Introduction to Spatial Econometrics
authors: LeSage, James P.; Pace, R. Kelley
methodology_summary: Comprehensive treatment of spatial autoregressive models (SAR, SEM, SDM, SLX). Introduces direct/indirect/total impacts decomposition for interpreting spatial spillover effects.
key_limitations: Assumes spatial weights matrix is correctly specified; limited guidance on weights selection

title: Review of Quantitative Methods for Supply Chain Resilience Analysis
authors: Hosseini, Seyedmohsen; Ivanov, Dmitry; Dolgui, Alexandre
methodology_summary: Comprehensive review classifying quantitative methods: Bayesian networks, simulation, optimization, control theory, complexity/reliability approaches for supply chain resilience.
key_limitations: Review paper; limited coverage of ML and spatial methods

title: A Quantitative Analysis of Disruption Risk in a Multi-Echelon Supply Chain
methodology_summary: Monte Carlo and discrete-event simulation for quantifying disruption risk across multi-echelon supply chains. Tests decentralized vs centralized inventory policies.
key_limitations: Simplified network topology; assumes independent failure events

title: Supply Networks and Complex Adaptive Systems: Control versus Emergence
authors: Choi, Thomas Y.; Dooley, Kevin J.; Rungtusanatham, M.
methodology_summary: Conceptual paper establishing supply chains as complex adaptive systems with properties of emergence, self-organization, and co-evolution.
key_limitations: Conceptual framework without empirical testing

title: Network Science Approach to Modelling the Topology and Robustness of Supply Chain Networks
authors: Perera, Sanja; Bell, Michael G.H.; Bliemer, Michiel C.J.
methodology_summary: Review applying complex network theory to supply chains. Covers network growth models, scale-free and small-world properties, robustness analysis via percolation.
key_limitations: Limited empirical validation of network models against real supply chain data

title: OR-Methods for Coping with the Ripple Effect in Supply Chains During COVID-19 Pandemic
methodology_summary: Combines discrete-event simulation with optimization for modeling disruption propagation (ripple effect) in multi-tier supply chains during COVID-19.
key_limitations: Simulation scenarios simplified relative to real pandemic dynamics

title: Structural Measures of Resilience for Supply Chains
methodology_summary: Node percolation processes on production networks modeling supplier failures from exogenous shocks. Shows cascading failure sizes follow power laws. Defines structural resilience as largest shock before phase transition.
key_limitations: Theoretical model; validated on synthetic and stylized networks only

title: Causal Machine Learning for Supply Chain Risk Prediction and Intervention Planning
methodology_summary: First comprehensive framework applying causal ML (treatment effect estimation, counterfactual prediction) to supply chain risk, enabling what-if scenario analysis for interventions.
key_limitations: Limited empirical validation; requires strong causal assumptions

title: LLM-Grounded Explainable AI for Supply Chain Risk Early Warning via Temporal Graph Attention Networks
methodology_summary: Combines temporal graph attention networks with LLM-generated explanations for supply chain risk early warning. Uses SHAP and GNNExplainer for post-hoc interpretability.
key_limitations: Novel architecture without broad empirical benchmarking

title: Semiconductor Supply Chain Resilience and Disruption: A Comprehensive Review
methodology_summary: Systematic review of semiconductor supply chain resilience covering concentration risks, disruption case studies (COVID, Texas freeze, Renesas fire), and mitigation strategies.
key_limitations: Review paper; limited quantitative analysis of concentration metrics

title: COVID-19 and Pharmaceutical Supply Chain Disruptions: Lessons and Implications
methodology_summary: Multi-method analysis of COVID-19 impacts on pharmaceutical supply chains: API shortages, PPE supply failures, demand forecasting breakdowns. Survey of pharmaceutical executives combined with supply chain mapping.
key_limitations: Survey conducted during acute crisis; recall bias; limited to firms willing to participate

title: Assessing the Vulnerability of Food Supply Chains to Climate Change-Induced Disruptions
methodology_summary: Scoping review of 67 selected papers from 1,526 examining food supply chain vulnerability to climate change. Covers production, processing, distribution, and retail stages.
key_limitations: Most reviewed studies focus on production stage only; limited coverage of processing and distribution

title: Towards Food Supply Chain Resilience to Environmental Shocks
methodology_summary: Cross-disciplinary review identifying research gaps in food supply chain resilience to environmental shocks. Covers agricultural production through consumption.
key_limitations: Conceptual framework; limited empirical validation

title: Geopolitics of the Energy Transition: Critical Materials
methodology_summary: Geopolitical analysis of critical mineral supply chains for clean energy. Maps geographic concentration, refining dominance, and supply security risks for lithium, cobalt, rare earths, copper, and graphite.
key_limitations: Projections depend on technology adoption scenarios; limited firm-level data

title: Deliver Uncompromised: A Strategy for Supply Chain Security and Resilience in Response to the Changing Character of War
methodology_summary: Analysis of 41 documented supply chain attacks on defense systems. Identifies three interconnected supply chains (global commercial, DoD acquisition, DoD sustainment) and proposes risk-based SCRM framework.
key_limitations: Defense-specific context; classified information gaps; US-centric

title: The Ever Given Grounding and Global Supply Chain Disruption: A Network Analysis
methodology_summary: Network analysis of the 6-day Suez Canal blockage (March 2021). Estimates $9.6 billion/day in delayed trade. Maps disruption propagation through maritime shipping networks.
key_limitations: Single event case study; short-duration disruption may underestimate chronic chokepoint risks

title: Measuring Economic Policy Uncertainty
authors: Baker, Scott R.; Bloom, Nicholas; Davis, Steven J.
methodology_summary: Constructs text-based Economic Policy Uncertainty index from newspaper archives. Trade policy uncertainty component measures tariff and trade agreement uncertainty.
key_limitations: Text-based measurement subject to media bias; English-language newspaper focus

title: Global Critical Minerals Outlook 2025
methodology_summary: Annual market analysis of critical mineral supply, demand, and concentration. Production and refining data from national geological surveys and industry databases.
key_limitations: Government and industry self-reported data; projections sensitive to technology adoption assumptions

title: Global Risks Report 2024 — Supply Chain Dimensions
methodology_summary: Expert survey of 1,490 risk professionals combined with scenario analysis. Supply chain disruption rated as one of the top short-term global risks.

title: Supply Chain Interdependence and Geopolitical Vulnerability: The Case of Taiwan and High-End Semiconductors
methodology_summary: Tabletop exercise with expert participants assessing geopolitical scenarios. Wargaming of China-Taiwan contingencies and impact on semiconductor supply.
key_limitations: Scenario-based exercise; outcomes depend on assumed geopolitical trajectories

title: Dual Gravity: Using Spatial Econometrics to Control for Multilateral Resistance
authors: Behrens, Kristian; Ertur, Cem; Koch, Wilfried
methodology_summary: Applied spatial autoregressive techniques to the gravity model of bilateral trade. Uses SAR specifications to capture third-country effects (multilateral resistance).
key_limitations: Cross-sectional analysis; spatial weights specification sensitivity

title: Online Detection of Supply Chain Network Disruptions Using Sequential Change-Point Detection for Hawkes Processes
methodology_summary: Sequential change-point detection for supply chain networks using Hawkes processes for event cascade modeling and modified CUSUM for real-time disruption detection.
key_limitations: Simulation-validated only; requires high-frequency event data
